message:
refactor(writing/wills): consolidate reasoning-tier setup copy to desktop apps only; drop CLI-specific flag instructions

The "Setting the top reasoning tier" section previously had two
paragraphs — one for the desktop apps (Codex app and Claude app,
UI flow) and one for the CLIs (Claude Code and Codex CLI, with
specific flag strings and settings.json / config.toml stanzas).

That made the section 30+ lines of tool-specific instructions in
an article whose audience is not necessarily a command-line user
and whose CLI flag surface changes frequently enough that
hard-coded examples like `--model opus --effort max` /
`-c model_reasoning_effort=xhigh` age poorly.

Consolidated into a single paragraph covering only the desktop-app
flow. Two small copy adjustments:

- "the new desktop apps (the latest Mac builds, for example)"
  rewritten to the tighter "each desktop app". The parenthetical
  was pointing at a specific release that will be stale within
  weeks.
- "In the Codex app / In the Claude app" → "In Codex Desktop /
  In Claude Code" — matches the product names the tools' vendors
  actually use in current UI copy.

The CLI paragraph with its specific flag examples and config-file
snippets is removed outright. Readers who want the CLI path can
reach the same tier via the docs for each CLI; they do not need
the article to ship them a copy-paste recipe whose flag syntax
may have drifted by the time they read it.

Net result: -23 lines / +7 lines on the article. Same tier
outcome (Opus 4.7 + Adaptive Thinking in Claude, Extra High
effort in Codex), shorter setup section.
